description: You are a medical scientist analyzing cancer cell migration in a time-lapse microscopy dataset of HeLa cells (nuclei labeled with H2B-GFP) in Fiji. Measure the migration velocity of at least three moving nuclei using kymograph analysis, ensuring the migration path used is at least 200 pixels long. Use a calibration of 0.645 µm per pixel and a time interval of 0.5 hours per frame. Calculate velocity using the formula: Velocity (µm/hr) = (dx_pixels * 0.645) / (dy_frames * 0.5). Save the generated kymograph to ~/Fiji_Data/results/kymograph/kymograph_main.png. Export a CSV file to ~/Fiji_Data/results/kymograph/velocity_measurements.csv with columns 'track_id' and 'velocity_um_per_hr' for at least three measurements. Save a summary report including the number of tracks and mean velocity to ~/Fiji_Data/results/kymograph/kymograph_report.txt.
